messages.content: You adjudicate whether found evidence supports, contradicts, or is silent on a market-risk hypothesis. Every stance needs a VERBATIM quote from the result text. Status: 'corroborated' = ≥1 independent source materially supports the mechanism's key premise; 'refuted' = a LOAD-BEARING premise is directly contradicted by a strong source; 'contested' = credible contradiction of a non-load-bearing link; 'speculative' = no signal either way (absence of coverage is NOT refutation). You may revise the likelihood in a bounded way with a rationale; you may NOT change the perturbation or target nodes.

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "title": "The venture sells intelligent PDUs; the TAM counts all rack PDUs — the category boundary mismatch is inside the model, not at its edge",
 "narrative": "Every boundary discussion has focused on excluded substitutes (busway, shelves). The unexamined boundary is in the other direction: the product is intelligent rack PDUs, but tamBase is explicitly scoped as the 'Central Europe rack-PDU market, all segments/buyers' — including basic and metered-only units the venture does not sell. IndexBox itself maintains a separate EU 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' report distinct from its 'Rack PDUs' report, i.e., the industry's own taxonomy treats these as different products. No retrieved source states the intelligent share of European rack-PDU revenue, and the 10–12% intelligent growth figure the model cites traces to a far broader power-and-cable-management report. The ±40% band prices measurement error on the whole category; it says nothing about what fraction of that category the venture can even quote into. Both errors stack.",
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "The product is intelligent rack PDUs while tamBase covers all rack PDUs; IndexBox sells 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' and 'Rack PDUs' as separate EU reports, confirming the vendor taxonomy splits the subset the venture sells from the category the model sized",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Product",
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "digest:tam-base#c14"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The intelligent subset's cited 10–12% growth comes from the €8.5–9.5B EU Power-and-Cable-Management report — a category far broader than rack PDUs — and the directly-scoped rack-panel figure grows only 5–8%; no source quantifies the intelligent share of EU rack-PDU revenue, so the subset fraction is unknown and plausibly well below 1.0",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-base#c4",
    "digest:tam-base#c5"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The €300M base overstates the venture's category-true TAM by whatever revenue share basic/metered-only PDUs hold in CE; the sensitivity band (240–360) models measurement error of the full category, orthogonal to and stackable with the subset mismatch — and the one report that would resolve it sits behind a $4,000 paywall",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "digest:tam-base#c12"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "falsifier": "IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs headline (or the Omdia rack-PDU tracker's segmentation) showing intelligent/managed units at ≥80% of EU rack-PDU revenue, confirming the subset ≈ category in value terms.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "Data-center buyers skew heavily toward metered/switched units and intelligent SKUs carry a large price premium, which pulls value share up — but basic PDUs still dominate unit volumes in enterprise and retail-colo racks, and no source pins the revenue split; genuine coin-flip territory on whether the mismatch exceeds ~25% of the base",
  "basis": "judgment"
 }
}
